title: Check if Point Satisfies Equation
description: Determine whether an ordered pair lies on a line by substitution.
condition: Given linear equation and ordered pair $(x,y)$.
response: Substitute $x$ and check if resulting $y$ matches.
label: Rule / Plan
example: For $y=-2x+8$, test $(4,0)$: $-2(4)+8=0$, so point lies on line.

title: Compare Left and Right Sides for Equality
description: Determine whether the evaluated expression equals the given y-value to decide if a coordinate pair satisfies an equation.
condition: Given a substituted equation with a numerical left-hand side and a numerical right-hand side.
response: State whether the two sides are equal (true) or not equal (false).
label: Concept
example: For point $(6,0)$ in $y = 8 - 3x$: compute $8 - 3(6) = 8 - 18 = -10$. Since $0 \ne -10$, the sides are not equal.
notes: Determines whether a point is a solution to the equation.

title: Compare Slopes to Determine Same Growth Rate
description: Determine whether two linear relationships have the same rate of change by comparing their slopes.
condition: Given two linear relationships (equations or graphs) with identifiable slopes.
response: Conclude that the relationships have the same growth rate if and only if their slopes are equal.
label: Concept
example: If line ℓ has slope 4 and another line has equation $h = 4w + 3$, both have slope 4, so they grow at the same rate.
notes: Often supported visually by observing that the lines are parallel.

title: Compare Unit Rates from Multiple Representations
description: Determine and compare unit rates (slopes) from equations, tables, or graphs to decide which rate is greater or smaller.
condition: Given multiple proportional relationships represented as equations, tables, or graphs.
response: Compute each unit rate (slope) and identify which is largest or smallest.
label: Principle / Model
example: From $d=9.5t$, slope $=9.5$. From table, $16\div2=8$. From graph using $(0,0)$ and $(30,300)$, slope $=300\div30=10$. Since $8<9.5<10$, the second plane is slowest.

title: Compute Constant of Proportionality
description: Find the constant of proportionality from a pair of corresponding values in a proportional relationship.
condition: A proportional situation with corresponding values (x, y) is given.
response: Compute the ratio y/x (or equivalent) to determine the constant of proportionality k.
label: Principle / Model
example: If Jada takes $8$ steps when Noah takes $10$, then $k = \frac{10}{8} = \frac{5}{4}$.

title: Determine Slope from Graph
description: Calculate slope as rise over run using two points on a graphed line.
condition: Given a graph of a non-vertical line with at least two identifiable points.
response: Compute slope as $(y_2-y_1)/(x_2-x_1)$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Evaluate Linear Expression with Substitution
description: Substitute a numerical value for a variable in a linear expression and simplify using order of operations.
condition: Given a linear expression in one variable (e.g., $8 - 3x$) and a numerical value for the variable.
response: Compute the numerical value of the expression.
label: Principle
notes: Requires correct use of multiplication before subtraction.

title: Graph a Proportional Relationship
description: Represent a proportional relationship on a coordinate plane as a straight line through the origin using an appropriate scale.
condition: An equation of the form y = kx (or equivalent proportional relationship) is given.
response: Plot (0,0) and additional points satisfying the equation, then draw a straight line through them.
label: Production / Skill
example: For $n = \frac{5}{4}j$, plot $(0,0)$ and $(100,125)$ and draw the line through them.

title: Graph Line from Slope and Initial Value
description: Graph a linear relationship given an initial value and a constant rate of change.
condition: Given an initial value (y-intercept) and a constant rate of change (slope).
response: Plot the y-intercept and use the slope to generate additional points, then draw the line.
label: Production / Skill
example: Given initial height 3 inches and growth 4 inches per week, plot $(0,3)$, then $(1,7)$, $(2,11)$, and draw the line through them.
notes: Requires coordinating slope as rise over run on a graph.

title: Identify Horizontal Line as Constant
description: Recognize that a horizontal line has slope 0 and represents a constant value.
condition: Given a graph of a horizontal line.
response: State slope is 0 and the quantity does not change.
label: Concept
example: Line $m$ is horizontal, so slope $=0$ and downloads remain constant.

title: Identify Solution to Linear Equation
description: Recognize that a coordinate pair is a solution to a linear equation if substituting its x- and y-values makes the equation true.
condition: Given a linear equation in two variables and a coordinate pair $(x, y)$.
response: Conclude that the point lies on the line if substitution makes the equation true; otherwise conclude it does not.
label: Principle
example: Check $(0,8)$ in $y = 8 - 3x$: $8 - 3(0) = 8$. Since $y = 8$, the equation is true, so $(0,8)$ lies on the line.
notes: Encapsulates the definition of a solution to an equation in two variables.

title: Identify Y-Intercept from Graph
description: Determine the y-value where a line crosses the y-axis.
condition: Given a linear graph.
response: State the y-value at x = 0.
label: Category
example: From the graph, when x = 0, y = 10000. The y-intercept is 10000.
notes: Requires recognizing the vertical axis intersection.

title: Interpret Rate from Proportional Equation
description: Identify the constant of proportionality as the rate of change in a proportional equation.
condition: An equation in the form y = kx is given in context.
response: State that k represents the rate per unit of x.
label: Concept
example: In $y = 3x$, the coefficient $3$ means 3 buckets per minute.

title: Interpret Slope as Rate of Change
description: Interpret slope in context as the rate at which one quantity changes per unit of another.
condition: Given a linear relationship in context (equation, table, or graph).
response: Explain slope as change in dependent variable per 1 unit of independent variable.
label: Principle / Model
example: In $y=10+4.5x$, slope $4.5$ means each sandwich adds $\$4.50$ to total cost.

title: Model and Solve Linear Constraint
description: Form and use a linear equation representing a total constraint from weighted quantities.
condition: Given item weights and a total capacity constraint.
response: Write equation (e.g., $40w+16t=2000$) and find solution pairs.
label: Principle / Model
example: If jugs weigh 40 lb and cases 16 lb with 2000 lb limit, equation is $40w+16t=2000$.

title: Parallel Lines Have Equal Slopes
description: Recognize that parallel lines have the same slope and differ only in intercept.
condition: Given two lines described as parallel.
response: Conclude their slopes are equal.
label: Concept
example: If the second store’s graph is parallel to $y=10+4.5x$, its slope is also $4.5$.

title: Use Proportional Equation to Predict Value
description: Substitute a given value into a proportional equation to determine the corresponding value.
condition: A proportional equation and a specific input value are given.
response: Substitute the input and compute the corresponding output.
label: Principle / Model

title: Write Equation for Proportional Relationship
description: Express a proportional relationship between two variables using an equation of the form y = kx or equivalent.
condition: A context describes two quantities varying proportionally.
response: Write an equation relating the variables using the constant of proportionality.
label: Principle / Model
example: Since $k = \frac{5}{4}$, the relationship can be written as $n = \frac{5}{4}j$ (or $j = \frac{4}{5}n$).

title: Write Equation of Vertical Line
description: Recognize that a vertical line has equation of the form $x=c$.
condition: Given a vertical line on a graph crossing the x-axis at a constant value.
response: Write equation $x=c$.
label: Rule / Plan
example: A vertical line through $x=1$ has equation $x=1$.

title: Write Linear Equation in Slope-Intercept Form
description: Construct a linear equation in the form $y = mx + b$ from a given slope and y-intercept.
condition: Given a constant rate of change (slope) and an initial value (y-intercept).
response: Write an equation of the form $y = mx + b$ substituting the given slope and intercept.
label: Principle / Model
example: Slope $4$ and initial value $3$ gives $h = 4w + 3$.
notes: Relies on the general linear model $y = mx + b$. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies]
